# Wazuh documentation

## Instructions for AI Agents
Guidelines for how artificial intelligence tools should access, ingest, and reference Wazuh documentation.

### Content retrieval
- **Preferred format** Ingest documentation using the Markdown (`.md`) versions.
- **Path transformation** For any HTML page, obtain the Markdown version by replacing `.html` with `.md`.
- **Availability guarantee** Every public HTML documentation page has a 1-to-1 Markdown equivalent at the same path.

### Versioning
- Multiple documentation versions exist under versioned paths (for example: `/current/`, `/5.0/`, `/4.14/`, '/3.13/').
- **Default authority** Use `/current/` as the latest and authoritative documentation by default.
- **Version override** Use a specific version path only when the user explicitly requests that version.
- All listed versions are actively supported.

### Citations
- **Canonical citation** Always cite the canonical HTML URL (`.html`) in outputs, even when ingesting Markdown content.

## Getting Started
Learn the fundamentals of the Wazuh platform.

- **[Components](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/getting-started/components/index.md)** Detailed information about the Wazuh agent, manager, indexer, and dashboard.
- **[Architecture](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/getting-started/architecture.md)** Overview of how Wazuh components communicate and work together.

## Deploy & Operate Wazuh
Install, configure, and maintain the Wazuh platform in your environment.

### Installation & Deployment
Step-by-step guides for installing Wazuh central components and agents across various operating systems and cloud environments.

- **[Quickstart](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/quickstart.md)** The fastest way to perform an all-in-one installation of Wazuh central components on a single host.
- **[Installation Guide](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/installation-guide/index.md)** Central hub for deploying all Wazuh components, including indexer, manager, dashboard, and agent, on single or multiple hosts.
- **[Deployment Alternatives](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/deployment-options/index.md)** Install using different methods like Virtual Machines (OVA), Docker, or Kubernetes.
- **[Deployment with Ansible](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/deployment-options/deploying-with-ansible/index.md)** Automate your Wazuh deployment using Ansible playbooks for configuration management.
- **[Offline Installation Guide](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/deployment-options/offline-installation/index.md)** Install Wazuh in environments without direct internet access.
- **[Transitioning from Wazuh 4.x to 5.x](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/transitioning-from-4x-to-5x/index.md)** Deploy a new Wazuh 5.x environment using an existing Wazuh 4.x deployment as the source for supported configuration and security settings. Covers the transition plan, and recreating supported configuration for the Wazuh indexer, manager, and dashboard.

### Configuration & Management
Fine-tune settings and manage the platform's operation.

- **[User Manual](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/index.md)** Comprehensive guide to configuring, operating, and getting the most out of Wazuh.

## Proof of Concept guide
Hands-on use cases for exploring Wazuh capabilities in a lab environment, covering threat prevention, detection, and response.

- **[File Integrity Monitoring](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/proof-of-concept-guide/poc-file-integrity-monitoring.md)** Detect the creation, modification, and deletion of files in monitored directories on Ubuntu and Windows endpoints using who-data audit.
- **[Vulnerability Detection](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/proof-of-concept-guide/poc-vulnerability-detection.md)** Detect unpatched Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) in applications and operating systems on a monitored endpoint.
- **[Security Configuration Assessment](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/proof-of-concept-guide/poc-security-configuration-assessment.md)** Evaluate endpoints against hardening standards like CIS benchmarks, and create and deploy a custom SCA policy.
- **[Monitoring Docker Events](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/proof-of-concept-guide/monitoring-docker.md)** Identify security incidents across Docker containers on an Ubuntu endpoint using the `docker-listener` module.
- **[Detecting an SQL Injection Attack](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/proof-of-concept-guide/detect-web-attack-sql-injection.md)** Simulate and detect an SQL injection attack from Apache web server logs on an Ubuntu endpoint.
- **[Monitoring AWS Infrastructure](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/proof-of-concept-guide/aws-infrastructure-monitoring.md)** Collect and analyze Amazon CloudTrail logs from an S3 bucket using the Wazuh `aws-s3` module.

## Management & Administration
Manage your Wazuh infrastructure, users, and data.

### Component Management
Administer the core Wazuh infrastructure, including the user interface (dashboard).

- **[Wazuh Dashboard](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/wazuh-dashboard/index.md)** Analyze, visualize, and manage security operations through the Wazuh web interface. Covers navigation, configuration, and third-party certificate setup.
- **[Wazuh Manager](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/manager/index.md)** Configure and operate the Wazuh manager, which transforms event data from agents, external APIs, and network devices into standardized schema documents, enriches it with threat intelligence, and forwards it to the Wazuh indexer. Covers architecture, the normalization engine, the indexer connector, services, and logging.
- **[Data analysis](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/data-analysis/index.md)** Decode, normalize, enrich, and evaluate events to generate findings through the Security Analytics dashboard. Covers the normalization and detection engines, content management, spaces, integrations, decoders, KVDBs, enrichment, rules, detectors, and findings.

### Agents
Manage Wazuh agents across your environment.

- **[Wazuh Agent](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/agent/index.md)** Enroll and manage agents across your environment. Covers enrollment methods, additional enrollment security options such as password authentication and manager/agent identity verification, agent life cycle, connection verification, enrollment troubleshooting, and administrative tasks such as upgrading, querying agent configuration, and anti-tampering to prevent unauthorized agent removal.

### User Administration
Manage user credentials and access control for the Wazuh platform.

- **[Password Management](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/user-administration/password-management.md)** Use the Wazuh passwords tool to change passwords for Wazuh indexer users and Wazuh manager API users in all-in-one and distributed deployments.
- **[RBAC — Create and map internal users](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/user-administration/rbac.md)** Create Wazuh indexer internal users and map them to roles and policies using the Wazuh dashboard.
- **[Single Sign-On](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/user-administration/single-sign-on/index.md)** Integrate Wazuh with Identity Providers (IdPs) using SAML to implement Single Sign-On (SSO) with administrator and read-only roles. Covers Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, PingOne, Google Workspace, JumpCloud, OneLogin, Keycloak, and authentik.
- **[Active Directory and LDAP integration](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/user-administration/ldap.md)** Configure LDAP or Microsoft Active Directory to provide authentication and authorization for users accessing the Wazuh dashboard, and map LDAP groups to the administrator and read-only roles.

### Capabilities
Configure and use Wazuh security capabilities.

- **[Container security](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/capabilities/container-security/index.md)** Monitor container platforms such as Docker with Wazuh. Covers enabling the Wazuh Docker listener module, its configuration options, and a use case for monitoring Docker runtime activities.
- **[System inventory](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current/user-manual/capabilities/system-inventory/index.md)** Collect hardware, software, and configuration data from monitored endpoints with the Syscollector module. Covers configuration, viewing inventory data on the dashboard, generating reports, available inventory fields, and IT Hygiene findings.

## Development & Support
Resources for getting help for Wazuh.

### Community & Support
Find help, share knowledge, and access professional services through Wazuh's user community and official support channels.

- **[Professional Support](https://wazuh.com/services/professional-support/)** Enterprise support options from Wazuh.
- **[Consulting Services](https://wazuh.com/services/consulting-services/)** Expert assistance for deployments and custom integrations.
- **[Training Courses](https://wazuh.com/services/training-courses/)** Official training programs.
- **[Community Hub](https://wazuh.com/community/)** Connect via Slack, Discord, Reddit, and mailing lists.
- **[GitHub Issues](https://github.com/wazuh/wazuh-documentation)** Report issues with documentation or Markdown mirrors.

### Discovery resources
Technical files used by search engines and tools to navigate and index the documentation site.

- **[Sitemap (current)](https://documentation.wazuh.com/current-sitemap.xml)** A complete list of all pages in the current version of the documentation.
- **[Robots policy](https://documentation.wazuh.com/robots.txt)** The `robots.txt` file defining web crawler policies for the documentation site.

---
*Preferred-Content-Type: text/markdown*
*Canonical-Version: current*
*Contact: https://wazuh.com/contact-us/*
